methodsAttempted.query: site:uoguelph.ca (ChatGPT OR "generative AI" OR "artificial intelligence" OR "AI tools") (policy OR guidelines OR academic integrity OR teaching OR privacy) "University of Guelph"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned official University of Guelph teaching, news-statement, AVPA, experiential-learning, and library guide pages related to generative AI.
Robots.txt allows the public OTL guidance path fetched here; admin/search/login paths are disallowed and were not accessed.
Robots.txt allows the public U of G News statement URL fetched here; /wp-admin and other disallowed paths were not accessed.
Robots.txt allows the AVPA and Experiential Learning public paths fetched here; disallowed search and user paths were not accessed.

Robots.txt allows the public guide path for generic crawlers while disallowing several named AI crawlers; this worker used Firecrawl and one direct public fetch without accessing disallowed search or redirect paths.

Robots.txt disallows userfiles and libraries but not /ai-guidelines. Firecrawl accessed the public guideline page; direct HTTP with the crawler user agent returned an empty 404 and was treated as a fetch limitation.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found central University of Guelph sources for teaching and learning, academic integrity, student guidance, and information security. A unit-specific Experiential Learning Hub AI page was recorded as official but limited because it addresses career-development/job-search use rather than university-wide academic policy.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Guelph has public official guidance pages for generative AI in teaching and learning, including an AVPA resource page and Office of Teaching and Learning provisional recommendations.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI in Teaching and Learning
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For University of Guelph courses, acceptable student use of AI is determined by the course instructor and may vary by discipline, program, and assessment type.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Guelph Office of Teaching and Learning says generative AI plagiarism detection software is not currently approved for use at U of G and instructors should not submit student work to generative AI detection tools while more is being understood.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow source-status claim based on the official AVPA resource page.
Approved because the claim follows the official statement's scope and does not overstate a blanket permission or ban.
Approved because the source directly states the academic-misconduct consequence for AI-completed materials without instructor permission.
Approved as a narrow academic-integrity claim about GenAI detection tools, not a broader software procurement claim.
Approved as a staff/faculty information-security and privacy claim based on the official IT Help guidance.
Approved as student-facing guidance; the claim stays scoped to the official library guide and instructor/course requirements.

artifacts.title: University of Guelph Generative AI Policy Evidence
artifacts.summary: Official University of Guelph sources show public guidance for generative AI in teaching and learning, instructor-scoped acceptable use in courses, academic-misconduct treatment for unauthorized AI-completed submissions, guidance against current GenAI detector use, and information-security cautions about sensitive University data and PII in AI tools.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The staged claims are agent-reviewed candidates only and are not marked canonical.
The Experiential Learning Hub AI page was not used for central university-wide policy claims because it is limited to career-development and job-search contexts.
Direct HTTP for the IT Help AI guidelines returned an empty 404 with the crawler user agent; Firecrawl successfully retrieved the public page and the limitation is recorded.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
